reasoning: Given the provided context, the Manifold market priced the probability of another >5% single-day decline in the S&P 500 at 11% on October 30, 2025. Between October 30 and the knowledge cutoff of November 9, 2025, search results confirm that the S&P 500 remained stable and trended upwards, closing at around 6,728 on November 7. A government shutdown and trade tensions that elevated the VIX in October showed signs of progress/stabilization in early November. With roughly 52 calendar days remaining in the year (down from 62 days on Oct 30), a linear time decay of the 11% probability puts the baseline at approximately 9.2%. Factoring in the recent positive news, record highs, normalized volatility, and the historical rarity of >5% daily drops in non-crisis periods, I am slightly adjusting this down further to 8%.
